text:
It's on the calendar. "On ******".

It began unexpectedly.

In her late teens, Olivia was hanging out at an aunt's quaint farmhouse. They had just had some tea together. As the afternoon sun dipped low in the sky, they talked about everything under the sun.

"One day," Aunt Clara said contemplatively, "I'll have a garden."

Olivia laughed.

"You're way too late for that."

"You think?"

She looked up from her knitting. They were admiring a rosebowl.

"Aunt Clara?"

"That would be me. Rose gardening."

"How?"

"They're my passion."

"Oh...?"

"And I want to grow roses all over that place. Ever."

"I could help."

"You do?"

"I'm pretty good."

Five years later...

She could whistle while she worked; they were beautiful.

One rainy afternoon, she was weeding next to a great big bush. It seemed endless.

"No way," she whispered.

Olivia found a cluster.

You couldn't
